name: Pin ℂ to a specific object. Make  compute the tuple from ℂ's data. Show the 17.28M addresses are realized, not merely available. 3³×4⁵×5⁴ is the size of the grid — the codomain. For Crystal = ℂ/~ you need the projection onto: every address inhabited by a real object of ℂ. Resolve the decode∘encode dilemma — the keystone. Fix only this and the centerpiece becomes a real theorem. Turn the folding/unfolding table into one proof. Force the value-counts, or name the ouroboros decoration.

phase_0.domain_name: Pin ℂ to a specific object. Make  compute the tuple from ℂ's data. Show the 17.28M addresses are realized, not merely available. 3³×4⁵×5⁴ is the size of the grid — the codomain. For Crystal = ℂ/~ you need the projection onto: every address inhabited by a real object of ℂ. Resolve the decode∘encode dilemma — the keystone. Fix only this and the centerpiece becomes a real theorem. Turn the folding/unfolding table into one proof. Force the value-counts, or name the ouroboros decoration.

VINIT.justification: the bare abstract category before any projection; objects and morphisms exist but are unindexed, voide of 12-tuple addresses.

TANCH.justification: the closed boundary of the 17.28M address space, sealing the system so every object maps into the 3³×4⁵×5⁴ codomain.

AFWD.justification: forward morphism sending each object X in ℂ to its 12-tuple equivalence class address.

AREV.justification: reverse morphism recovering the equivalence class representative from a 12-tuple address.

CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of limits, colimits, adjunctions, monoidal products etc. as folding/unfolding moves on the 12-tuple.

IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference; the object that recognizes its own 12-tuple — the identity survives the quotient and certifies decode∘encode=id.

FSPLIT.justification: splits each categorical object X into two arms — 'limit exists and colimit is universal' (true arm) vs. 'limit diverges or colimit fails' (false arm).

FFUSE.justification: multiplies the true and false arms to recover the original object X exactly; limit verdict adds no new information, merely classifies the fiber.

EVALT.chosen_element: convergent fiber — limit/colimit exist and are universal
EVALT.justification: affirmative state: X inhabits a 12-tuple address where its structural limits are satisfied.

EVALF.justification: false state: X inhabits a 12-tuple address where its structural limits diverge; this fiber is still an inhabited equivalence class.

ENGAGR.justification: both arms simultaneously active — an operation that is both a forward morphism and its reverse, held without resolution; the dialetheic fixed point that makes the 11-state inhabited.

IFIX.justification: permanent, append-only encoding of the 12-tuple into the 17.28M address lattice — irreversible fixation of the fiber index.

phase_2.fuse_result: X exactly, because existence of a limit is a property of X already encoded in its fiber — the 12-tuple does not change.

phase_3.void_description: ℂ as uninitialized void — no object yet assigned a 12-tuple address (register 00).
phase_3.true_description: object X classified in convergent fiber — its limit structure is satisfied (register 01).
phase_3.false_description: object X classified in divergent fiber — its limit structure fails (register 10).
phase_3.both_description: dagger structure: X with an involution that is simultaneously a forward and reverse morphism — dialetheic fixed point (register 11).

steps.domain_action: bare abstract category ℂ exists as uninitialized void — objects and morphisms are raw, no 12-tuple addresses assigned.
apply crystal_encode projection π to a chosen object X ∈ ℂ — compute its 12-tuple.
fork on limit-existence for X — true arm if limit exists and colimit is universal; false arm if limit diverges or colimit fails.
enter convergent fiber — X satisfies its structural limits; its 12-tuple is recorded in the true register.
enter divergent fiber — X fails its structural limits; its 12-tuple is recorded in the false register.
in convergent fiber, chain applicable folding operations (monoidal product, tensor, endofunctor iteration) that change the 12-tuple cross-fiber.
in divergent fiber, chain unfolding operations (natural transformations, coherence conditions) that stay within the fiber, invisible to the 12-tuple projection.
invoke dagger structure on X — X carries an involution † such that π(X) = π(X†); the object is simultaneously in the convergent and divergent register, a paradice.
multiply true and false arms — the limit verdict is erased, recovering the bare object X exactly.
apply crystal_decode to the address of X — lift the 12-tuple back to an equivalence class representative in ℂ.
Yoneda embedding verifies self-recognition — the object X recovered by decode∘encode is naturally isomorphic to the original X; identity survives the quotient.
seal the 12-tuple into the 3³×4⁵×5⁴ mixed-radix address lattice — the boundary of the crystal.
permanently record the inhabited address of X in the 17.28M space — this address is now realized, not merely available.
iterate the projection over all objects in ℂ — populate every fiber.
for each inhabited fiber, verify at least one witness object exists in ℂ.
final census — all 17,280,000 addresses are inhabited by at least one real object of ℂ; the projection is surjective onto the codomain.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: crystal_encode — the quotient map ℂ → Crystal that projects any categorical object/morphism onto its 12-tuple equivalence class.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: 12-primitive basis addresses shared as 27×1024×625 mixed-radix numerals; components exchange tuple addresses.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the 17.28M address lattice — each 12-tuple stored as a compact mixed-radix integer; fibers indexed by their address.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the 4-stage as_above_induction derivation: L1–L6 logical → I1–I4 inductive → A1–A5 algebraic, applied in sequence to crystallize primitives.
phase_5.alfs_store: the Lean-verified bijection crystal_decode ∘ crystal_encode = id — canonical append-only log-structured reference store guaranteeing round-trip faithfulness.

phase_6.cycle_cost: computation of limit/colimit existence; negligible structural change because the verdict is a fiber classification, not a modification.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: object X without its limit-verdict label.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: object X with its limit-verdict label, then recovered exactly by μ: FFUSE(FSPLIT(X)) = X.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the Frobenius round-trip is exact; no information is created or destroyed, only compressed into the 12-primitive fiber address.
